text.title: best-practices-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for best-practices-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Outdated React patterns inherited from training data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents emit React.FC, useEffect for data fetching in App Router pages, or wrap server components with useState. Reject and re-prompt with the RSC boundary explicit.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents quote best-practices-2026/README.md in PRs as if it were a vendor doc. Force agents to cite the underlying source URL or, better, the project constitution clause.

failure-mode.description: Stale dep pins from training cut-off.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents pin next: "13.x" because that was current in training. Force ncu post-generation and reject training-anchored versions.

failure-mode.description: Using any to escape type pressure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: any disables all type checking for the value and any values derived from it. Every any is a deferred runtime error.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default LLM output reaches for any under TypeScript pressure. Forbid via ESLint @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any and reject PRs that disable it inline without a reason.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents alternate between int | None and Optional[int] within a file. Pick one (preferably the modern syntax) and lint.

failure-mode.description: Adding 'use client' to a layout component to "just make it work".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This flips the entire subtree client-side. The fix cascades bundle size into every page under that layout.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents copy README snippets and skip the directive — function silently runs as a normal client function. Lint for the directive on every export named *Action or in actions.ts.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adding 'use client' to a layout flips the entire subtree. Agents do this to "just make it work"; reviewer must catch.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents call getProduct(params.id) twice (once in metadata, once in page). Memoize via cache(...) or shared module-level promise.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Renamed to useActionState in newer React; agents flip-flop. Pin React version + the matching API name in the constitution.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents read the README and recommend python -X nogil for production. It is experimental in 3.13. Block this in the constitution.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents create unbounded TaskGroups; in production they hang on a slow downstream. Mandate async with asyncio.timeout(...) around groups.

failure-mode.description: Accepting AI-generated test baselines in visual regression tools without human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent accepting its own baselines defeats the purpose of visual regression testing. The first baseline must always be approved by a human.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI test tools generate flaky XPath-anchored tests that break on first UI change. Always wrap with self-healing locators or migrate to role-based queries.
